Title: Lead Scientist
Description:
Ready to architect the future of human-computer voice interaction?




Join an established conversational AI company as they transition from traditional cascaded speech systems to cutting-edge E2E speech-to-speech technology. You'll lead this transformation, building multimodal systems that will redefine how millions interact with AI.




The opportunity

You'll be leading the development of speech technology that directly impacts real users at massive scale. The company processes millions of daily interactions across major enterprise clients, meaning your research will shape real-world conversational experiences.




You'll spearhead the development of full-duplex speech systems, creating truly natural AI conversations that go far beyond current capabilities.




Your impact

Design and build next-generation multimodal speech LLM architecture from the ground up
Drive breakthroughs in speech-to-speech modeling and full-duplex conversation systems
Tackle turn-taking, interruption handling, and simultaneous speech processing
Bridge cutting-edge research with enterprise-grade production systems
Lead a growing team focused on SOTA speech-to-speech breakthroughs and own the development end-to-end




What you'll bring

Deep understanding of SOTA speech models and neural audio processing
Experience building speech language models/multimodal systems
Strong background in speech AI research and modern speech architectures




This is all underpinned by access to a large corpus of real enterprise conversational data and serious GPU infrastructure.




The company has built everything in-house, giving you complete technical control and the freedom to explore any approach that delivers value.




With their established market position and proven track record, you'll have the resources and real-world testing ground to make transformative impact with your research.




Location

Remote (Must be within EU timezone).

Title: Medior Data Scientist
Description:
Are you passionate about #AI or #cybersecurity? You love working with high profile teams with a sure taste for challenge and variety? You dream to join a fast-growing company with start-up mentality? Eager to learn continuously? Join Proximus Ada! Within Proximus’ centre of excellence for AI and Cybersecurity, a team of data scientists, data engineers and analytics translators, supports the Proximus group in designing, developing and deploying internal AI-driven applications in line with the business needs.

As a Data Scientist, you will play a key role in developing end-to-end solutions for AI use cases. By taking up a use case right from the start you will help our customers define and scope the problem, pick the right ML solution and technology as well as ensure implementation, integration and deployment of your solution to production. You will do so while ensuring that our internal governance processes, technical excellence and best practices are respected. You will also collaborate with other team members in knowledge sharing as well as continuously improving our way of working.

Responsibilities


Develop AI applications using state-of-the-art (Gen)AI technology.
Translate business requests into AI applications.
Pilot prototypes in production processes to demonstrate their value.
Deploy prototypes to production to obtain reliable, scalable systems.
Present your results in a clear manner and discuss them with multi-functional project teams.
Work in close collaboration with business experts (e.g. for requirement gathering, data source identification, data and process understanding, feature engineering, result validation, etc.), and with other AI engineers in the team (e.g.forknowledgesharing).


Profile


PhD or Master's degree in Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
Proven experience (2+ years) in AI/ML engineering with a strong focus on deploying and integrating AI


Technical Skills


Strong expertise in Python
Strong knowledge of state-of-the-art AI and statistical methods
Proven proficiency with deploying scalable AI applications to production
Experience using LLM’s and commonly used libraries to interact with LLM’s (Langchain, Llamaindex, ...)
Hands-on experience with Azure Cloud, and Azure Data & AI services
Strong Expertise with version control (Git, GitHub)
Expertise with CI/CD pipelines, and modern software development best practices
Experience with Agentic AI frameworks (Langgraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, …) is a big plus
Experience with MLOps tools and frameworks (g. MLflow, Kubeflow, TFX) is a big plus


Attitudes/Behavior


Excellent problem-solving skills.
Collaboration: Strong interpersonal skills to work effectively with diverse stakeholders from data science, engineering, and business units.
Excellence: Strong commitment to excellence by consistently striving for high-quality outcomes, embracing continuous improvement, and maintaining a proactive, detail-oriented approach in all tasks.
Innovation Mindset: Willingness to explore new technologies and methodologies to drive continuous improvement.
Resilience: Capacity to troubleshoot and resolve issues in complex changing environments while maintaining a focus on quality and user experience.
Communication: Ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences and provide clear documentation.


Languages


Fluent in English + French and/or Dutch

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



What you will do

As an expert in the field of Machine Learning Engineering you will be responsible for the growth of our Data team, both professionally as well as in size. You will be in charge of shaping and executing the future of your expertise within Sia. You will work on client projects as well as on building our own Sia AI solutions, together with our Dutch team of Data Scientists, our French Data Engineering team, and other business units of Sia Global.



Your responsibilities include:



Leading an internal team towards the development of AI products;
Working in a multidisciplinary team on client projects as an expert on your subject;
Making architectural and design decisions for cloud environments;
Building production level ML/AI solutions with solid software engineering;
Guiding Data Scientists to scale up their models into production (parallelizing, optimizing, tuning, testing, and wrapping);
Giving trainings, leading internal meetings and organizing external events;
Participating in team meetings and workshops with clients;
Writing blog articles, presenting at conferences and sending out press releases on the subject that you lead.

Who you will work with

The Data Science team is young, diverse and growing (10+). You will join a team that will challenge you and invest in your professional development. We have a flat structure and rely on the entrepreneurial spirit of our members: initiative is highly encouraged. We are striving for well-being, diversity and knowledge-sharing inside the team, so that we can grow together.



Who you are



You are an established technical professional that wants to make the next step in their career, taking on more leadership responsibilities.
You have first-rate project management and time management skills.
You are a true problem solver, you turn complex issues into simple and impactful business solutions.
You are enthusiastic for applying Data Science/Engineering solutions to business problems.
You have excellent communication skills and can present complicated concepts in a synthetic way.
You have the ability to adapt rapidly and work on a range of different business areas within a challenging environment.
You have the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


MSc level education in Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems or related field of study.
2-4 years of experience in building production-quality ML/Deep Learning pipelines and models.
Good understanding of Machine Learning fundamentals and strong knowledge in a specific domain (e.g., Computer Vision, Deep Learning, NLP).
Expert coding skills in Python and at least one of these languages: Scala, C++, Java.
Hands-on experience with database management languages (e.g., SQL, PostgreSQL).
Hands-on experience in cloud-based infrastructures (AWS/GCP/Azure).
Hands-on experience with Unix-based command line and DevOps tools (Git, Docker, Kubernetes).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies (e.g. Spark, Hadoop, Databricks).
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Experience with coaching/mentoring other engineers.
Prior experience in Management Consulting is a strong plus.
Willingness to travel and work at local and international clients.
Fluency in spoken and written English, French and Dutch is required.


Additional Information



Unfortunately, we are unable to provide a work permit for this position.



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Don’t settle for just any consulting company, choose the one that’s right for you!




At Data Wizards, we value perspeverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.




On our side, we do our best to find exciting, challenging and meaningful projects for you. Today, we work with companies of all sizes, across various sectors, where we believe our work brings real value and purpose.




We are continuously on the lookout for bright data engineers, data scientists, machine learning engineers, AI engineers, and data analysts. But this job description is for Data Scientists out there feeling the magic in them and ready to spread it🪄




As a Data Scientist, your mission would include

Leading the technical aspects of data science projects, from conception to deployment.
Designing and implementing scalable data science solutions that integrate seamlessly with existing infrastructures.
Developing and optimizing machine learning models to drive actionable insights and automation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including data engineers and business stakeholders, to align technical solutions with strategic goals.
Taking ownership of projects, ensuring high-quality deliverables in an agile environment.
Providing technical leadership and mentorship to junior data scientists.
Engaging in proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scaling them into production-ready solutions.
Working in cloud-based environments and following MLOps best practices.
Communicating insights and technical solutions effectively to diverse audiences.




You’re a step closer to joining our team if you bring:




Background

Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, AI, or a related technical field.
5+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, or AI-driven projects.
Experience in diverse industries and solutions, demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving skills is considered as a plus.

Technical Skills

Strong programming skills in Python and SQL.
Expertise in machine learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies and cloud platforms (AWS preferred; Azure is a plus).
Experience deploying ML models and following MLOps best practices.
Understanding of data engineering principles to support scalable AI solutions.
Ability to design and implement proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scale them into production.

Soft Skills

Autonomous & Proactive: You take initiative and drive projects forward
Curious & Adaptive: You embrace learning and thrive in dynamic environments.
Solution-Oriented: You balance technical rigor with business impact.
Clear Communicator: You simplify complex ideas for diverse audiences.
Collaborative & Supportive: You work well in teams.
Creative Thinker: You find innovative solutions to challenging problems

Practical Info

Languages: Fluent in English. French or Dutch Native.




We know that finding the right job isn’t just about the role, it’s about what’s in it for you, too. While we’re a start-up, we’re big on taking care of our people right from day one.




Here’s a sneak peek at what we offer:

💰 A compensation package that keeps you motivated

Competitive salary
Mobility budget or company car with a fuel card
30 days of paid time off
A top-tier laptop & smartphone with a subscription
Representation allowances
Hospitalization, group & dental insurance
Meal vouchers & eco-vouchers
Opportunity to earn company stock over time




🎯 A place to be your best self, grow and thrive

Work with the most innovative technologies in Data & AI
Work in an agile, collaborative environment
Hybrid work model
Monthly team-building events (plus one epic annual celebration)
Tailored training programs to fuel your career growth
A cool, ergonomic office with creative meeting spaces
A Nintendo Switch for spontaneous Mario Kart battles 🎮
A massage chair for those deep-thinking moments 🧘
Fresh coffee & drinks to keep you energized ☕




✨ And most importantly, a growing company where opportunities are limitless!




Our Recruitment process:

Quick intro call with our HR team
HR Interview
Technical Interview
Partner Interview
Potential Interview with client




If you’re passionate about what we do but aren’t sure you meet every single requirement, don’t hesitate to apply. We value potential, mindset, and growth as much as experience. At Data Wizards, we believe diversity is our strength. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and experiences because different viewpoints help us grow and evolve.




Let’s build the future of AI & Data, together🤝




Please note: As a consulting company, our recruitment process can be either project-driven or based on strong profiles we believe in. The consultancy world moves fast, and while we always aim to match great talent with the right opportunity, there might not always be an immediate fit. With your permission, we’ll gladly keep your profile in mind for future missions, and hope to collaborate with you soon on an exciting project at Data Wizards!

Title: Software Engineer
Description:
Senior C++ Developer - Broadcast Playout & Real-Time Media Systems

Location: Remote (UK / EU)

Type: Contract (rolling / indefinite)

Start Date: June 2026




We’re looking for a Senior Software Developer to join a globally recognised broadcast technology company powering thousands of TV channels across more than 50 countries worldwide.




This is a critical hire within the core Development team, focused primarily on a flagship, mission-critical broadcast playout platform used by major broadcasters globally.




You’ll play a key role in designing and evolving complex real-time broadcast software, working across modern C++, video/audio processing, graphics pipelines, and mission-critical playout systems.




What you’ll do

Design, develop, and improve features across a flagship broadcast playout platform
Work on real-time video and audio processing within mission-critical broadcast environments
Investigate and resolve software issues using crash dumps, logs, and debugging tools
Contribute to modernising and improving a long-standing C++ codebase
Develop and maintain unit tests and software quality processes
Configure and maintain development, testing, and build environments
Collaborate closely with support, delivery, and product teams to solve complex customer challenges
Mentor and support junior developers within the team
Contribute to coding standards, architecture discussions, and engineering best practices
Take ownership across the full software development lifecycle, from requirements through to customer deployment




Technical environment

Modern C++ (C++11, C++17)
Qt Framework
Real-time broadcast video and audio processing
Signal-level broadcast technologies and graphics pipelines
Broadcast playout and channel automation systems
Crash analysis, debugging, and fault investigation
Client/server communication and distributed systems
SQL, XML, and network synchronisation
Build systems and software tooling
Exposure to Matrox SDKs/hardware beneficial




What we’re looking for

Strong experience as a Senior Software Developer working with modern C++
Hands-on experience with real-time video and/or audio processing systems
Background within broadcast, media technology, playout, or signal-processing environments
Commercial experience using the Qt framework
Strong debugging and crash analysis capabilities within complex software systems
Experience delivering software products through the full SDLC
Ability to work effectively within a fully remote engineering team
Strong analytical problem-solving and communication skills
Experience mentoring developers or providing technical leadership is highly desirable
Experience working with broadcast vendors, playout providers, or production technology companies
Knowledge of graphics processing, video pipelines, or hardware SDK integrations




If this sounds like you, please do get in touch!

  • Develop and maintain unit tests and software quality processes
  • Configure and maintain development, testing, and build environments
  • Collaborate closely with support, delivery, and product teams to solve complex customer challenges
  • Mentor and support junior developers within the team
  • Contribute to coding standards, architecture discussions, and engineering best practices
  • Take ownership across the full software development lifecycle, from requirements through to customer deployment


Technical environment

  • Modern C++ (C++11, C++17)
  • Qt Framework
  • Real-time broadcast video and audio processing
  • Signal-level broadcast technologies and graphics pipelines
  • Broadcast playout and channel automation systems

  • Client/server communication and distributed systems
  • SQL, XML, and network synchronisation
  • Build systems and software tooling

  • Strong experience as a Senior Software Developer working with modern C++
  • Hands-on experience with real-time video and/or audio processing systems
  • Background within broadcast, media technology, playout, or signal-processing environments
  • Commercial experience using the Qt framework
  • Strong debugging and crash analysis capabilities within complex software systems
  • Experience delivering software products through the full SDLC
  • Ability to work effectively within a fully remote engineering team
  • Strong analytical problem-solving and communication skills
  • Experience mentoring developers or providing technical leadership is highly desirable

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Senior Data Scientist (Python / ML / SQL / Cloud)

Remote (EU-based contractors)

€500/day

Immediate Start




We’re working with a globally recognised digital & technology-led organisation undergoing a major data transformation programme across its European operations.




This is not a pure research role.




You’ll be joining a high-performing data function where the focus is on building and deploying real-world decision models that directly impact commercial performance across digital and eCommerce channels.




The Project

This programme is centred around unlocking value from large-scale digital, retail, and media datasets, including complex third-party and privacy-safe data environments.

You’ll be responsible for taking new and existing data sources and turning them into:

Scalable decision models
Actionable insights for commercial teams
Logic that powers internal tools and workflows

The work sits at the intersection of data science, product, and commercial strategy — with a strong emphasis on impact and adoption, not just experimentation.




What You’ll Be Doing

Researching and onboarding new data sources (APIs, third-party datasets, clean-room environments)
Translating business problems into clear modelling and analytical approaches
Building decision models, forecasting frameworks, and recommendation logic
Applying ML/AI techniques where appropriate, with a focus on explainability and measurable outcomes
Working closely with BI and data engineering teams to productionise models
Supporting client-facing discussions, explaining methodology and driving adoption
Contributing to commercial work, including proposals and case studies




What They’re Looking For

8+ years in Data Science / Applied ML / Advanced Analytics
Strong Python for modelling and analysis (production-quality code)
Advanced SQL and experience working with large, complex datasets
Solid grounding in statistics, experimentation, and measurement
Experience building models that are actually deployed and used, not just prototypes
MLOps exposure (deployment, monitoring, retraining strategies)
Comfortable working cross-functionally with data engineering and BI teams
Strong communication, able to explain complex ideas clearly to non-technical stakeholders

Nice to have:

Experience in digital, eCommerce, retail media, or marketplace environments
Exposure to privacy-safe / clean-room data environments
Cloud data stack experience
NLP / GenAI applied to commercial use cases




Important

EU-based contractors only
Must have a registered company set up and ready to freelance
Immediate availability required




If you’re looking for a role where your models actually drive decisions, not just sit in notebooks, this is a strong one to get involved in.

Title: Software Developer (React/C#)
Description:
Onderneming




Deze start-up software onderneming te Kampenhout voorziet in een volledige implementatie van IT producten bij zowel kleine als grote ondernemingen. Ze maken het verschil door volledige en gedetailleerde analyses te maken betreffende verschillende ERP mogelijkheden om zo af te kunnen stemmen wat de meest optimale oplossing is.




Takenpakket




Als Software Developer werk je projectmatig aan de ontwikkeling van nieuwe applicaties, met een sterke focus op frontend development in React. Je analyseert de functionele en technische behoeften en vertaalt deze naar performante, gebruiksvriendelijke en schaalbare oplossingen.




Naast je focus op React krijg je de mogelijkheid om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen richting fullstack development. Op termijn krijg je de kans om ook backendtechnologieën zoals C# en Python onder de knie te krijgen en mee te werken aan het volledige developmentproces van frontend tot backend. Zo bouw je stap voor stap expertise op binnen het volledige softwarelandschap en neem je ownership over projecten van analyse tot oplevering.




Profiel




Je beschikt over een masterdiploma binnen IT, Burgerlijk Ingenieur, Industrieel Ingenieur of een gelijkaardige richting, of je behaalde een bachelordiploma aangevuld met meerdere jaren relevante ervaring in development.
Je hebt een sterk analytisch inzicht en haalt energie uit het analyseren, optimaliseren en uitwerken van technische oplossingen.
Je communiceert vlot, werkt graag samen en kan tegelijkertijd zelfstandig verantwoordelijkheid opnemen binnen projecten.
Je hebt een uitgesproken interesse in softwareontwikkeling en bouwt graag zelf toepassingen uit, bij voorkeur met technologieën zoals C# of Python.




Aanbod




Brutoloon naar ervaring
Bedrijfswagen + tank/laadkaart of mobiliteitsbudget
Hospitalisatieverzekering
Netto onkostenvergoedingen
Maaltijdcheques
3 dagen thuiswerk
Veel flexibiliteit
Werken in een bedrijf met een vlakke in-house structuur

Title: Senior Data Scientist - ML Engineer
Description:
About Devoteam:

Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital transformation. We help our clients leverage technology to achieve their business goals.

Our longstanding partnerships with leaders like AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and ServiceNow amplify our capabilities, allowing us to deliver top-tier expertise and award-winning services. We complement these partnerships with in-depth Cyber, Data & AI, Software development and multi cloud expertise.

For 30 years, we have always been guiding our customers on the next tech wave. Devoteam has guided organizations through the Internet and cloud. The next revolution? AI.

With 11,000 tech natives working in 25+ countries, we bring you highly skilled IT experts across Europe

At Devoteam, tech is in our DNA, and it’s the people we empower with tech. Now, with responsible AI, we’re ready to unlock a future where technology drives positive change.

Job Description

About the Role:

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Senior Data Scientist - ML Engineer to join our growing Data & AI team (50 FTE) in Belgium. In this role, you will play a key part in designing, developing, and deploying machine learning solutions for our clients across various industries. You will collaborate with other data scientists, engineers, and consultants to deliver innovative and impactful solutions that address our clients' business challenges.

Responsibilities:


Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models and algorithms
Analyze large and complex datasets to identify patterns and insights
Build and maintain data pipelines for data ingestion, processing, and transformation
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business requirements and translate them into technical solutions
Stay up to date with the latest advancements in machine learning and artificial intelligence
Contribute to the development of Devoteam's data science and machine learning capabilities



Qualifications

Qualifications:


Master's degree or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
5+ years of experience in a data science or machine learning role
Strong programming skills in Python or R
Experience with machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Experience with Databricks is a plus
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Fluent in English and proficiency in Dutch or French is mandatory.
Sponsorship for a work permit not possible



Bonus Points:


Experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark, or Hive
Experience with MLOps tools and practices
Experience with natural language processing or computer vision
Contributions to open-source projects or publications in relevant conferences or journals



Additional Information

Why choose us?


Thrive in a fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment
Work with innovative and impactful Cloud, Data & AI projects for industry-leading clients
Connect with extraordinary talents in a collaborative and diverse culture
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance in an inspiring, eco-friendly workplace
Competitive compensation package



At Devoteam, we combine strong values – respect, frankness, ambition, entrepreneurship & collaboration – with a fun and supportive environment that empowers you to innovate, grow, and succeed in the fast-paced world of Cloud, Data & AI.

Title: AI Specialist
Description:
About Us

We are a well-established Belgian company in the insurance sector. Our flagship product is a SaaS platform used by insurance brokers, backed by over 20 years of success and a highly efficient, robust codebase built primarily in .NET.

We are currently in a strategic phase of innovation, integrating Artificial Intelligence to revolutionize how our users interact with our platform. To achieve this, we are building an ecosystem of Python microservices that communicate with our core system via APIs, and we are looking to expand our AI team to accelerate this journey.

The Role

You will join a growing Artificial Intelligence team, working closely with our AI Specialist. Your primary mission will be to design, develop, and deploy Generative AI solutions that deliver direct value to our users and optimize internal processes.

This is not just an R&D or experimental role; we have an ambitious roadmap to launch these new AI features into production. Our SaaS is used daily by thousands of professionals as their primary work tool. This means you will tackle real-world challenges related to high volume, scale, and performance, taking your code from development straight to the end-user.

You will have end-to-end ownership of your development lifecycle, supported by our Infrastructure and DevOps team.

What you will do in your day-to-day

Develop and implement AI-driven features, such as intelligent email analysis, automated data extraction from documents, conversational assistants, and complex RAG systems.
Build and maintain efficient, scalable microservices in Python using FastAPI.
Apply Prompt Engineering techniques, build RAG architectures, and integrate LLMs using frameworks like LangChain and LangGraph.
Design and implement robust LLM evaluation pipelines to ensure the quality, accuracy, and reliability of our generative models in production, leveraging observability tools like LangFuse.
Collaborate closely with the .NET development team to ensure seamless integration of AI services via APIs.
Engage with technical and business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate user needs into technical solutions.
Explore and evaluate new AI technologies, with the potential to dive into model fine-tuning and traditional Machine Learning in the future.
Manage the deployment and monitoring of your services in Azure, taking end-to-end responsibility with the backing of our infrastructure team.

What we are looking for

Demonstrable Mid-level experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on Python and API development (FastAPI).
Hands-on experience working with Large Language Models (LLMs), RAG architectures, and Prompt Engineering.
Familiarity with the Generative AI ecosystem and tooling; specifically, practical knowledge of frameworks like LangChain/LangGraph and monitoring/evaluation tools like LangFuse.
Experience working with cloud providers, preferably Azure (and familiarity with tools like Azure AI Foundry).
A strong "get things done" and product-oriented mentality. You are driven by shipping features to production, adding tangible value to customers, gathering real-world feedback, and iteratively improving the product.
An inquisitive, adaptable mindset with a strong capacity to learn and stay continuously up-to-date with the latest trends, tools, and breakthroughs in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.
Excellent communication skills to interact effectively with various stakeholders across the company.

Nice-to-haves

Knowledge or familiarity with .NET/C# to better understand the core of our platform.
Previous experience with fine-tuning open-source models or traditional Machine Learning.
Familiarity with LLM evaluation methodologies.

The Role

You will join a growing Artificial Intelligence team, working closely with our AI Specialist. Your primary mission will be to design, develop, and deploy Generative AI solutions that deliver direct value to our users and optimize internal processes.

This is not just an R&D or experimental role; we have an ambitious roadmap to launch these new AI features into production. Our SaaS is used daily by thousands of professionals as their primary work tool. This means you will tackle real-world challenges related to high volume, scale, and performance, taking your code from development straight to the end-user.

You will have end-to-end ownership of your development lifecycle, supported by our Infrastructure and DevOps team.

What you will do in your day-to-day

  • Develop and implement AI-driven features, such as intelligent email analysis, automated data extraction from documents, conversational assistants, and complex RAG systems.
  • Build and maintain efficient, scalable microservices in Python using FastAPI.
  • Apply Prompt Engineering techniques, build RAG architectures, and integrate LLMs using frameworks like LangChain and LangGraph.
  • Design and implement robust LLM evaluation pipelines to ensure the quality, accuracy, and reliability of our generative models in production, leveraging observability tools like LangFuse.
  • Collaborate closely with the .NET development team to ensure seamless integration of AI services via APIs.
  • Engage with technical and business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate user needs into technical solutions.
  • Explore and evaluate new AI technologies, with the potential to dive into model fine-tuning and traditional Machine Learning in the future.
  • Manage the deployment and monitoring of your services in Azure, taking end-to-end responsibility with the backing of our infrastructure team.

What we are looking for

  • Demonstrable Mid-level experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on Python and API development (FastAPI).
  • Hands-on experience working with Large Language Models (LLMs), RAG architectures, and Prompt Engineering.
  • Familiarity with the Generative AI ecosystem and tooling; specifically, practical knowledge of frameworks like LangChain/LangGraph and monitoring/evaluation tools like LangFuse.
  • Experience working with cloud providers, preferably Azure (and familiarity with tools like Azure AI Foundry).
  • A strong "get things done" and product-oriented mentality. You are driven by shipping features to production, adding tangible value to customers, gathering real-world feedback, and iteratively improving the product.
  • An inquisitive, adaptable mindset with a strong capacity to learn and stay continuously up-to-date with the latest trends, tools, and breakthroughs in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.
  • Excellent communication skills to interact effectively with various stakeholders across the company.

Nice-to-haves

  • Knowledge or familiarity with .NET/C# to better understand the core of our platform.
  • Previous experience with fine-tuning open-source models or traditional Machine Learning.
  • Familiarity with LLM evaluation methodologies.

Title: QA Engineer
Description:
QA Engineer




Brussels | Hybrid




Own quality on complex financial implementations




At Craft it, we help financial institutions solve complex problems at the intersection of credit risk, regulation, data, and technology.




As a focused team, we work with large international financial institutions across Europe, the US, and beyond.




This means you will own real client deliveries : designing test strategies, building automation, validating implementations, and being the QA point of contact on your workstream.




Our work is hands-on and closely tied to delivery. Quality is not a separate phase : it is part of how we build, configure, and release.




We are looking for a QA Engineer with solid testing experience, strong autonomy, and the ability to bring structure to complex environments. You will work directly with senior consultants, clients, and our QA Architect, and you will help our junior QA engineers grow.




What you will do




You will own quality on assigned workstreams and contribute to the practice as it grows. You should be comfortable working independently while collaborating closely with project teams and clients.




You will be responsible for:




designing test strategies for client implementations (functional, regression, UAT)
writing and maintaining automated tests in Playwright with TypeScript
contributing to and improving our test automation framework and practices
leading test execution cycles end-to-end, from planning to validation
being the QA point of contact for your workstream, with both internal teams and clients
preparing and validating test data, including direct database queries (SQL)
conducting API-level testing where relevant
producing clear, structured bug reports and defect analysis
documenting test results, coverage, and risks in a way clients can act on
supporting and reviewing the work of junior QA engineers
contributing to release validation and go/no-go decisions




You will also deepen expertise in:




banking and financial services
credit risk and regulatory frameworks (Basel, IFRS 9)
enterprise testing strategies (functional, regression, integration, performance)
modern test automation patterns, tooling, and CI/CD integration




What we are looking for




You are a strong fit if you:

have 3–7 years of QA experience, including hands-on automation
can autonomously own a testing scope from planning to release validation
are structured, rigorous, and bring clarity to ambiguous situations
communicate clearly and professionally with both engineers and clients
take ownership of outcomes, not just tasks
are fluent in English and French




You should be solid , not just familiar ,with:




core testing concepts and strategies (functional, regression, UAT, integration)
JavaScript or TypeScript, with hands-on Playwright experience (or equivalent modern automation framework : Cypress, Selenium, Playwright in another language and willingness to move to Playwright + TypeScript)
SQL and database-level validation
API testing (REST, JSON, occasionally XML)
delivery tools such as Azure DevOps
Git and basic CI/CD workflows
structured problem-solving and root-cause analysis




Experience in financial services, regulated industries, or complex enterprise software is a strong plus. So is exposure to mentoring or supporting more junior engineers.




Your first months




month 1: onboarding to our tools, framework, and active project context; getting embedded in your first workstream
months 2–3: owning a defined test scope, contributing to the automation framework, and starting to act as QA point of contact on your project
after 3–6 months: leading test cycles end-to-end on your workstream, mentoring junior QA engineers, and contributing to QA practice improvements alongside the QA Architect




You will work directly with our QA Architect and have regular structured feedback.




What you will get




a meaningful role in a growing QA practice, with direct impact on how we operate
ownership of real client deliveries from day one
hands-on work with modern automation (Playwright, TypeScript) and the freedom to improve the framework
a clear progression path toward QA Lead
exposure to a domain (financial services, credit risk) where QA depth has real career value
direct collaboration with senior consultants, clients, and the QA Architect




Why join Craft it




a focused team of 25 people
a growing QA practice with a clear progression path (QA Engineer → QA Lead → QA Architect)
work on complex topics with large international clients
real proximity to clients and project outcomes
a structured environment with high expectations
a company committed to responsible business practices (B-Corp certification)




Practical details




hybrid model: 3 days/week in the office
Brussels-based, with international client exposure




A flexible compensation package,including:




a mobility budget tailored to your needs
monthly homeworking allowance
monthly internet allowance
meal vouchers (Monizze)
Eco vouchers (Monizze)
comprehensive hospital insurance (DKV)
group pension insurance




Recruitment process




initial screening call
two interview rounds
a technical exercise during the second round (e.g. reviewing a test strategy, writing or refactoring an automated test, or analysing a defect scenario)







Interested?

Send us your CV and a short note explaining why this role is a strong fit for you.

https://www.craft-it.io/

Craft it is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability or any other characteristic.

Description:
SNI is serving as a trusted IT Outsourcing partner in line with the needs of World's most prestigious firms and carried out successful projects worldwide




www.sniconsulting.net




For one of our clients based in Europe, we are currently looking for a Senior Data Scientist.




Details:




Start date: ASAP
Contract Duration: 6 months with extension
Remote work with occasional on-site team sessions in Prague
US hours overlap: Min. 2–6 pm CET (ideally until 7 pm CET)

Design, train and deploy transformer-based AI models for text and image applications.

Fluent English and strong communication abilities.

Title: Data Scientist Engineer
Description:
Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital transformation. We help our clients leverage technology to achieve their business goals.

Our longstanding partnerships with leaders like AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and ServiceNow amplify our capabilities, allowing us to deliver top-tier expertise and award-winning services. We complement these partnerships with in-depth Cyber, Data & AI, Software development and multi cloud expertise.

For 30 years, we have always been guiding our customers on the next tech wave. Devoteam has guided organizations through the Internet and cloud. The next revolution? AI.

With 11,000 tech natives working in 25+ countries, we bring you highly skilled IT experts across Europe

At Devoteam, tech is in our DNA, and it’s the people we empower with tech. Now, with responsible AI, we’re ready to unlock a future where technology drives positive change.

Job Description

Are you passionate about data-driven platforms, machine learning models, and innovative applications? As a Data Scientist/Engineer at Devoteam, you'll play a hands-on role in delivering impactful solutions across various clients and industries. Each mission we undertake has clear deliverables and deadlines, ensuring you gain diverse experience. For example, one month you might develop a predictive maintenance model, and the next, a customer segmentation model for a retail client.

Key Responsibilities:


Platform Setup: Design and implement robust data platforms using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud and Databricks.
Data Management: Gather, prepare, and manage data for analysis.
Model Research: Investigate and select appropriate machine learning models.
ML Modeling: Develop and refine machine learning models.
Client Collaboration: Spend approximately 50% of your time working directly with clients, either independently or as part of a team.



Your Role:


Build and maintain data platforms and pipelines using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud, Databricks, and Python.
Develop predictive and machine learning models tailored to client needs.
Advise clients on translating their business requirements into technical solutions using machine learning techniques.
Stay updated on the latest trends in machine learning and AI, sharing insights with clients.
Act as a strategic partner to client Data Science teams, helping them navigate their challenges and aspirations.
Inspire clients with new data-driven opportunities.
Bring best practices in Git, DevOps, and CICD to enhance client data platforms.
Collaborate with Data Scientists, Data Engineers, and Business Stakeholders.
Represent Devoteam both online and offline, showcasing our expertise.



Qualifications

About You:


Reside in Belgium. (Mandatory)
Hold a university degree in Computer Science, (Business) Engineering, or a specialized master's in Machine Learning or AI.
Minimum 3 years experience in a similar role.
Have a solid understanding of various machine learning models (e.g., SVM, RNN, Deep Learning) and their applications.
Possess foundational knowledge of Data Science, including sampling techniques and evaluation metrics.
Proficient in SQL and Python.
Experience with Spark and/or cloud ecosystems is a must.
Experience with Databricks is real asset
Eager to learn and passionate about data and machine learning technologies.
Excited to join a dynamic and growing team.
Fluent in English; proficiency in Dutch or French is mandatory
Sponsorship for a work permit not possible



Additional Information

Why choose us?


Thrive in a fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment
Work with innovative and impactful Cloud, Data & AI projects for industry-leading clients
Connect with extraordinary talents in a collaborative and diverse culture
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance in an inspiring, eco-friendly workplace
Competitive compensation package



At Devoteam, we combine strong values – respect, frankness, ambition, entrepreneurship & collaboration – with a fun and supportive environment that empowers you to innovate, grow, and succeed in the fast-paced world of Cloud, Data & AI.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Project description:




Function description:

ML Engineers contribute to Machine Learning projects by:

• Working with the Data Scientists to define and develop the target solution with production constraints in mind. This allows to select the correct run infrastructure and serving model (e.g. data ingestion scheme, API synchronicity, …) to address the business requirements (real-time responses, processing volumetry, …)

• Contributing to the automation of the different elements of the ML pipeline in order to integrate and deploy them in the production environment (e.g. building Docker/VM images, prepare unitary, regression and integration tests, …)

• Supporting Data Scientists on the usage of the existing industrial solutions available to build and monitor AI services (i.e. the CI/CD tools)

• Supporting IT Production on the parameterization of the target environment Ensuring that the model runs without errors, is retrained if needed (incl. automatically) and is monitored both from the IT and the business perspective.




Required experience / Knowledge:

At least 4 years of relevant experience Technical experience:




Mandatory:

• Containerization / Virtualization

• AI platforms & IDEs

• CI/CD (Gitlab-ci)

• Code, model & data versioning

• Python advanced

• Usage of package management tools and experience in dependency management

• PostgreSQL

• Knowledge of agile methodology




Preferable:

• Experience with integration using different technologies (distributes/mainframe) and infra components

• Model compression techniques

• ELT / ETL tools

• Big data tools (Spark)

• Data flow processing

• Data visualization tools Business experience:

Title: Software Engineer
Description:
The role

You'll be the first software engineering hire at Gridual. You'll work across the full backend stack, owning real systems that control physical assets in a live market. This isn't a role for someone who wants a clear ticket queue and a comfortable lane. It's for someone who gets energy from figuring things out, moves fast without cutting corners on what matters, and wants to look back in a few years and say they built something real.

 

What you'll work on

Low-latency data pipelines feeding forecasting and real-time control systems
Backend services that integrate with energy markets and partner APIs
Observability and reliability for systems where downtime has real consequences
The architecture decisions that will shape how we scale

 

What you'll bring

Ambition and boldness: ready to tackle complex, high-impact problems
Strong software engineering skills, with Python-heavy backend experience
Experience building robust, production-ready data pipelines or backend systems
A hands-on, ownership mindset. You ship code fast, and are ready to leverage AI-based tools to do so

Bonus points:

Cloud infrastructure experience (AWS, GCP)
Experience with schedulers, or real-time data
Energy, trading, or high-reliability domain experience

 

Why join us?

No bullshit: we're a small team acting fast
Competitive compensation package including equity
By building critical systems to control physical assets, your impact will be immediate and substantial

 

Convinced?

Drop us a message at careers@gridual.ai before May 31.

Title: Data Scientist - GenAI
Description:
Join Artefact Belgium: Shape the Future of Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Are you an early-career professional excited to harness the power of generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)? Artefact Belgium is seeking a Data Scientist - Gen AI to join our innovative team in Brussels. Our Belgian team specializes in LLM and GenAI projects, and this is your chance to work on cutting-edge solutions, collaborate with top-tier experts, and contribute to impactful projects in a dynamic, forward-thinking environment.

Who You Are

A recent graduate or professional with 1 to 3 years of experience, with hands-on experience working with LLMs and a passion for generative AI and NLP. Motivated to apply your technical expertise to real-world challenges in AI. Able to thrive in collaborative settings while independently driving your own workstreams. Eager to learn, grow, and push the boundaries of what's possible in AI innovation.

Who We Are

Artefact is a leading Data & AI consulting firm operating at the forefront of AI innovation. With a global presence, we help clients across industries—public health, manufacturing, etc. —leverage generative AI to achieve transformative outcomes. Our team in Belgium is specialized in LLM and GenAI projects, working on the most advanced applications of these technologies. At Artefact, we foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, with a strong commitment to inclusivity and employee growth.

What You Will Do


Collaborate with the team to design, train, and fine-tune state-of-the-art models for client projects and internal tools.
Develop and integrate agentic AI solutions using tool calling, and emerging protocols like Model Context Protocol (MCP) to create autonomous, context-aware applications.
Research and implement innovative generative AI applications to solve complex business problems.
Design and implement comprehensive evaluation frameworks to measure LLM performance, establishing quality metrics and benchmarks that guide development decisions and ensure reliable, production-ready solutions.
Optimize the performance, scalability, and usability of LLM-powered products and tools.
Contribute to the deployment and maintenance of AI systems, including models, APIs, and supporting infrastructure.
Participate in technical discussions and brainstorming sessions to shape impactful solutions.


What You Bring


A degree in computer science, mathematics, engineering, physics, or a related field.
Experience working with LLMs, generative AI, or advanced NLP applications (e.g., fine-tuning models, using frameworks like Hugging Face or OpenAI).
Strong programming skills in Python and familiarity with AI and NLP libraries.
Analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with an ability to address real-world challenges through AI.
Good communication skills in English, Dutch or French; additional languages are a plus.
A growth mindset, adaptability, and eagerness to work in a fast-paced, cutting-edge environment.


What We Offer

HTML:
Join Artefact Belgium: Shape the Future of Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Are you an early-career professional excited to harness the power of generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)? Artefact Belgium is seeking a Data Scientist - Gen AI to join our innovative team in Brussels. Our Belgian team specializes in LLM and GenAI projects, and this is your chance to work on cutting-edge solutions, collaborate with top-tier experts, and contribute to impactful projects in a dynamic, forward-thinking environment.

Who You Are

A recent graduate or professional with 1 to 3 years of experience, with hands-on experience working with LLMs and a passion for generative AI and NLP. Motivated to apply your technical expertise to real-world challenges in AI. Able to thrive in collaborative settings while independently driving your own workstreams. Eager to learn, grow, and push the boundaries of what's possible in AI innovation.

Who We Are

Artefact is a leading Data & AI consulting firm operating at the forefront of AI innovation. With a global presence, we help clients across industries—public health, manufacturing, etc. —leverage generative AI to achieve transformative outcomes. Our team in Belgium is specialized in LLM and GenAI projects, working on the most advanced applications of these technologies. At Artefact, we foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, with a strong commitment to inclusivity and employee growth.

What You Will Do

  • Collaborate with the team to design, train, and fine-tune state-of-the-art models for client projects and internal tools.
  • Develop and integrate agentic AI solutions using tool calling, and emerging protocols like Model Context Protocol (MCP) to create autonomous, context-aware applications.
  • Research and implement innovative generative AI applications to solve complex business problems.
  • Design and implement comprehensive evaluation frameworks to measure LLM performance, establishing quality metrics and benchmarks that guide development decisions and ensure reliable, production-ready solutions.
  • Optimize the performance, scalability, and usability of LLM-powered products and tools.
  • Contribute to the deployment and maintenance of AI systems, including models, APIs, and supporting infrastructure.
  • Participate in technical discussions and brainstorming sessions to shape impactful solutions.

What You Bring

  • A degree in computer science, mathematics, engineering, physics, or a related field.
  • Experience working with LLMs, generative AI, or advanced NLP applications (e.g., fine-tuning models, using frameworks like Hugging Face or OpenAI).
  • Strong programming skills in Python and familiarity with AI and NLP libraries.
  • Analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with an ability to address real-world challenges through AI.
  • Good communication skills in English, Dutch or French; additional languages are a plus.
  • A growth mindset, adaptability, and eagerness to work in a fast-paced, cutting-edge environment.

What We Offer

  • A competitive salary with benefits tailored for early-career professionals.
  • Hands-on experience with state-of-the-art LLM technologies in impactful projects.
  • A dynamic and supportive work environment that fosters creativity and professional development.
  • Resources for ongoing training, certification, and skill-building in generative AI and related fields.
  • A collaborative, inclusive workplace with access to global expertise through Artefact's network.

This position is based in Brussels.

Don't worry if you don't meet every criterion! We value passion, potential, and a commitment to innovation. If you're excited about generative AI and ready to grow your career, we'd love to hear from you!

Title: Talent Acquisition Partner (Life Sciences Department)
Description:
We are the reference partner for the best companies in Europe.

Located across the entire territory, we have developed our expertise in three key business lines: Engineering, IT & Life Sciences. To strengthen its services, ALTEN Belgium is actively looking for a Talent Acquisition Partner to further develop the Life Sciences division.

The Talent Acquisition Partner plays a key role within the organization. It is a position with strong responsibilities, directly contributing to the company’s growth. We are looking for a dynamic professional to help strengthen our organization.

Come and meet us—we’ll tell you everything! If you’re up for the challenge, keep reading and discover this exciting opportunity.




Your role

Under the responsibility of the Recruitment Manager, you will play a key role in the recruitment process of our future Life Sciences consultants and engineers. You will oversee several missions within ALTEN Belgium.




Recruitment

In collaboration with Business Managers, you will define needs and write job offers to be posted on the appropriate platforms
Identify candidates through job boards, social networks, and various sourcing channels to attract top talent
Conduct initial phone screenings and pre-qualifications
Manage the full recruitment cycle: from interview scheduling with Business Managers to contract signing
Actively participate in weekly operational meetings




Communication & Employer Branding

Support and develop relationships with universities and schools
Attend job fairs and recruitment events
Organize recruitment-related events




Profile & Requirements

Strong interest in recruitment and the Life Sciences sector
Excellent communication and organizational skills
Languages: Dutch (mandatory), English (fluent), French is a plus
Location : This position is based primarily in Brussels, with regular presence required in Antwerp. You will work four days per week from our Brussels office and one day per week from our Antwerp office, ensuring close collaboration with both teams.

Title: AI Engineer (CDI, freelance)
Description:
Amarris Tech (groupe Amarris, +700 collaborateurs en France et Belgique) entre dans une nouvelle phase de son histoire : l'intégration de l'IA au cœur de sa plateforme technologique propriétaire.

Nous recherchons un(e) AI Engineer passionné(e) par la construction d'agents IA, prêt(e) à concevoir et déployer des systèmes intelligents qui transforment concrètement la façon dont nos clients et collaborateurs travaillent au quotidien.

🎯 Ce que tu feras

En Tant Qu'AI Engineer, Tes Missions Incluront Notamment


Concevoir et développer des agents IA capables d'exécuter des tâches complexes de façon autonome, en s'appuyant sur nos données métier.
Intégrer et orchestrer des LLMs (GPT, Claude, Mistral, etc.) dans des pipelines robustes, indépendants d'un modèle spécifique.
Construire et maintenir des APIs pour connecter nos agents à notre écosystème de données et de logiciels comptables.
Concevoir des architectures RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) exploitant nos bases de données métier : factures, écritures comptables, données financières.
Mettre en place des mécanismes d'évaluation et de garde-fous pour garantir la fiabilité des agents en production.
Collaborer étroitement avec les comptables seniors et chefs de projet pour traduire les besoins métier en solutions IA concrètes.
Contribuer à la construction de l'OS IA d'Amarris : infrastructure souveraine, scalable et indépendante des fournisseurs LLM.


📌 Profil recherché

Nous Recherchons Un(e) Candidat(e) Qui


A au moins 1 ans d'expérience en IA générative ou en développement d'agents et a moins 4 ans d'expérience en développement.
Maîtrise ou une connaissance des frameworks d'orchestration d'agents (LangChain, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI ou équivalents).
Est à l'aise avec les concepts de RAG, de vector stores (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector…) et de gestion de prompts.
Comprend les enjeux de souveraineté des données et sait concevoir des architectures respectueuses de la confidentialité.
Est curieux(se), autonome, et aime apprendre vite dans un domaine qui évolue à toute vitesse.


💼 Ce que nous offrons


Un contrat CDI (ou un statut freelance) au sein d'une équipe tech en forte croissance (35+ ingénieurs), à Bruxelles.
Une infrastructure propriétaire riche en données métier sur laquelle construire des agents vraiment utiles — pas de PoC sans lendemain.
Un rôle fondateur au sein d'une équipe IA en cours de constitution, avec une vraie influence sur les choix d'architecture.
Une politique de travail hybride flexible (télétravail + présence en bureau).


📈 Pourquoi ce rôle est stratégique

Ce poste est idéal pour une personne qui :


Veut construire des agents IA en production réelle, pas juste expérimenter dans des notebooks.
Veut travailler sur de la donnée métier dense et structurée, là où l'IA crée une valeur ajoutée immédiate et mesurable.
Cherche à poser les bases d'une infrastructure IA qui sera déployée à l'échelle d'une entreprise de +700 personnes.

Title: (Digital) Marketing Coordinator BeLux
Description:
Mission of the Role:

As a (Digital) Marketing Coordinator BeLux, you will play a key role in launching and establishing our brand across the Belgium and Luxembourg. You will ensure that our (digital) marketing presence is locally relevant, culturally accurate, compliant and commercially effective—while supporting the overall execution and optimization of our digital marketing activities.




Key Responsibilities:

Localize and adapt marketing content (website, CRM campaigns, paid media assets) for Belgium and Luxembourg, with a strong focus on both Walloon, Luxembourg (French) and Flemish (Dutch) audiences.
Create and build email campaigns (CRM), including copywriting, segmentation, and basic performance tracking.
Manage and update website content via CMS, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and SEO best practices.
Support execution of paid media campaigns (e.g., social, display, search), including asset coordination and performance monitoring.
Analyze campaign performance and provide actionable insights and recommendations for optimization.
Collaborate with central/global teams to adapt international campaigns to local market needs.
Ensure brand tone of voice and positioning are consistently reflected across all digital touchpoints.
Assist in testing, learning, and iterating digital marketing approaches during the market entry phase.




Your Profile:

Fluency in French (Walloon/Luxembourg market) and Dutch (Flemish market) is essential; professional proficiency in English required.
2–5 years of experience in digital marketing, CRM, content, or a related field.
Hands-on experience with email marketing tools and/or CMS platforms.
Basic understanding of paid digital media (Meta, Google Ads, or similar platforms).
Strong attention to detail, especially in localization and content quality.
Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and translate it into actions.
Comfortable working in a dynamic, fast-growing, and pioneering environment.
Experience with automotive and automotive communication compliance is a strong plus.
Proactive, structured, and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously.




What We Offer:

Competitive remuneration package.
A fast-paced dynamic environment in a successful international organization.
International exposure with the chance to adapt locally and make a difference.
Growth potential.

Title: Bioinformatician In Cancer Biology
Description:
Position

The Cancer Ecosystems and Metastasis Laboratory (CEMLab) at the Université Libre de Bruxelles (ULB), led by Panagiotis (Takis) Karras, is seeking for a skilled bioinformatician with experience in single-cell multi-omics. The candidate will focus on projects to better characterize the drivers of cancer metastasis, profile the inter- and intra-tumor heterogeneity and plasticity combining mouse models and clinical samples. Applicants should demonstrate expertise in programming languages (Python and/or R) as well as experience working with high-performance computing clusters. The successful applicant will join the ULB Faculty of Medicine and the Institute Jules Bordet within the campus.




About CEMLab

CEMLab investigates the cellular and molecular mechanisms driving the metastatic cascade and explores how the tumor ecosystem evolves in primary and metastatic organs. Our goal is to develop a comprehensive understanding of the molecular mechanisms and cell-cell interactions that favor metastasis and promote tumor outgrowth in distant organs.




Profile

We are seeking for motivated candidates having the following requirements:

·    Received a MSc in Bioinformatics or PhD within 4 years (or are about to graduate from a PhD program) in Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, or a related field of biology.

·    For postdoc position have first-authored original publication(s) in high-quality peer-reviewed journals.

·    Experience with high-throughput omics technologies such as gene expression transcriptomics, proteomics, epigenomics etc.

·    Demonstrated experience in cancer biology.

·    Proficiency in Python and/or R.

·    Experience with high-performance computing clusters and single-cell omics data analysis and integration.

·    Ability to proactively manage and drive projects.

·    Collaborate with wet lab scientists to refine and integrate datasets.

·    Strong written and oral communication skills.  




What we offer

·    A 4-year funded PhD position or full-time 2-year contract (for postdoc level), with the possibility of extension.

·    Stimulating research environment promoting quality, professionalism, and teamwork.

·    Opportunity to work on a team in close collaboration with physicians.

·    Possibility to attend training courses to learn and implement new bioinformatics pipelines.

·    Immediate incorporation.

·    Flexible working hours and measures to reconcile work, family and personal life.

·    The salary will be commensurate according to experience following ULB’s pay scale.




Want to join us?

Interested candidates should send their application via email to Panagiotis Karras at panagiotis.karras@ulb.be, including a cover letter detailing relevant skills and experiences and a CV for assessment. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. Early submissions are encouraged as the position is available for immediate start.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
AI Engineer

Brussels · On-Site

SaaS Startup




We are working with a fast-growing SaaS startup transforming the legal domain with modern technology—They’re looking for an AI Engineer who wants to build, own, and shape core product systems from the ground up.




This is a role where you can have real ownership and impact on a product that is revolutionising the legal and finance industry within a company that is growing at an incredible pace.




With expansion plans into wider Europe and the US, the Sky really is the limit with your own career growth.




What you’ll be doing

Designing, building, and scaling core features of their SaaS platform
Build production-ready LLM agents with multi-step reasoning and tool use
Design and implement agentic workflows using Pydantic AI
Integrate LLMs (Claude, Gemini, OpenAI) into customer-facing features
Building robust backend services and APIs with Python & FastAPI




What They’re looking for

Strong experience as an AI Engineer or in a similar role working with AI agents
Solid backend experience with Python, ideally FastAPI
A product mindset: you care about users, not just code
Comfortable taking ownership, making decisions, and moving fast
Based near (or willing to relocate to) Brussels




Bonus points if you have experience with

Working professionally in a SAAS Start-up environment
Building quickly and having great ownership




Why join Them?

High ownership & autonomy – your work will directly shape the product and company
Real impact – we’re solving meaningful problems in the legal and financial domains
Modern tech stack with room to influence architectural decisions
Ambitious, smart team that values pragmatism, quality, and speed
Up to €5k gross - Dependent on ability and experience
Mobility Budget
Meal Vouchers
Equity Options
Hospitalisation Insurance




If you’re an ambitious engineer who wants to build something that matters—and doesn’t want to be boxed into a narrow role—we’d love to hear from you!

Title: Data-analist
Description:
Consultant- Data Analyst (NL) - Exsolvæ – Brussel en omgeving (op locatie)




Over Exsolvæ

Exsolvæ is een Brussels consultancybureau gespecialiseerd in Data en Artificial Intelligence. Wij begeleiden onze partners bij complexe, vaak unieke uitdagingen via consultance, audits en het ontwikkelen van op maat gemaakte oplossingen. Onze aanpak is bewust holistisch: we laten Data Engineering, Advanced Analytics en AI samenkomen, want wij geloven erin dat daar de échte impact ontstaat.

Bij Exsolvæ noemen we onze consultants Solvers; gepassioneerde professionals die met de nieuwste technologieën werken, blijven leren, kennis delen, en gedreven zijn om duurzame oplossingen met écht resultaat te leveren.




Over de rol

Als Data Analyst speel je een sleutelrol in het omzetten van data naar inzichten die het verschil maken bij strategische beslissingen. Je werkt nauw samen met business- en technische teams om sterke datamodellen te ontwerpen, krachtige dashboards te bouwen, en ervoor te zorgen dat data binnen de organisatie correct, veilig en efficiënt wordt verwerkt.




Wat ga je doen?

Datamodellen ontwerpen, ontwikkelen en optimaliseren die de business en strategie ondersteunen
Power BI dashboards bouwen en uitbouwen die complexe data omzetten naar heldere, bruikbare inzichten
Werken met grote datasets, met aandacht voor performance, schaalbaarheid en snelheid
Data-transformaties optimaliseren via Power Query, DAX en SQL best practices
Databricks inzetten voor data preparation, transformatie en modellering
Werken met data in cloudomgevingen zoals Azure en/of AWS om analyses en rapporteringen op te zetten
Data security (RlS/OLS) implementeren en beheren; de juiste toegang voor de juiste mensen
Kwantitatieve en statistische analyses uitvoeren om inzichten te genereren die onderbouwde beslissingen ondersteunen
Bijdragen aan data-standaarden, governance en best practices binnen de organisatie
Mee bouwen aan datakwaliteit, consistentie en betrouwbaarheid




Wat breng je mee?

4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analyst of in een gelijkwaardige rol
Sterke expertise in Power BI, zowel in dashboard design als in performance optimalisatie
Gevorderde kennis van SQL en DAX, met focus of efficiënte queries en berekeningen
Hands-on ervaring met Databricks
Ervaring in Azure- en/of AWS-omgevingen
Bewezen ervaring met tabular models en high-performance analytics
Solide kennis van datamodellering (star schema, dimensional modeling)
Ervaring met role-based security (RLS/OLS)
Een analytische mindset en een gestructureerde aanpak van problemen
Sterke communicatieve skills; je kan complexe data vertalen naar heldere verhalen
Vloeiend Engels en Nederlands en idealiter spreek je ook een woordje Frans (B1/B2)




Waarom Exsolvæ?

Werken aan projecten met écht impact
Een innovatieve omgeving waarin leren en kennisdeling centraal staan
Volop kansen om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen

HTML:
Consultant- Data Analyst (NL) - Exsolvæ – Brussel en omgeving (op locatie)


Over Exsolvæ

Exsolvæ is een Brussels consultancybureau gespecialiseerd in Data en Artificial Intelligence. Wij begeleiden onze partners bij complexe, vaak unieke uitdagingen via consultance, audits en het ontwikkelen van op maat gemaakte oplossingen. Onze aanpak is bewust holistisch: we laten Data Engineering, Advanced Analytics en AI samenkomen, want wij geloven erin dat daar de échte impact ontstaat.

Bij Exsolvæ noemen we onze consultants Solvers; gepassioneerde professionals die met de nieuwste technologieën werken, blijven leren, kennis delen, en gedreven zijn om duurzame oplossingen met écht resultaat te leveren.


Over de rol

Als Data Analyst speel je een sleutelrol in het omzetten van data naar inzichten die het verschil maken bij strategische beslissingen. Je werkt nauw samen met business- en technische teams om sterke datamodellen te ontwerpen, krachtige dashboards te bouwen, en ervoor te zorgen dat data binnen de organisatie correct, veilig en efficiënt wordt verwerkt.


Wat ga je doen?

  • Datamodellen ontwerpen, ontwikkelen en optimaliseren die de business en strategie ondersteunen
  • Power BI dashboards bouwen en uitbouwen die complexe data omzetten naar heldere, bruikbare inzichten
  • Werken met grote datasets, met aandacht voor performance, schaalbaarheid en snelheid
  • Data-transformaties optimaliseren via Power Query, DAX en SQL best practices
  • Databricks inzetten voor data preparation, transformatie en modellering
  • Werken met data in cloudomgevingen zoals Azure en/of AWS om analyses en rapporteringen op te zetten
  • Data security (RlS/OLS) implementeren en beheren; de juiste toegang voor de juiste mensen
  • Kwantitatieve en statistische analyses uitvoeren om inzichten te genereren die onderbouwde beslissingen ondersteunen
  • Bijdragen aan data-standaarden, governance en best practices binnen de organisatie
  • Mee bouwen aan datakwaliteit, consistentie en betrouwbaarheid


Wat breng je mee?

  • 4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analyst of in een gelijkwaardige rol
  • Sterke expertise in Power BI, zowel in dashboard design als in performance optimalisatie
  • Gevorderde kennis van SQL en DAX, met focus of efficiënte queries en berekeningen
  • Hands-on ervaring met Databricks
  • Ervaring in Azure- en/of AWS-omgevingen
  • Bewezen ervaring met tabular models en high-performance analytics
  • Solide kennis van datamodellering (star schema, dimensional modeling)
  • Ervaring met role-based security (RLS/OLS)
  • Een analytische mindset en een gestructureerde aanpak van problemen
  • Sterke communicatieve skills; je kan complexe data vertalen naar heldere verhalen
  • Vloeiend Engels en Nederlands en idealiter spreek je ook een woordje Frans (B1/B2)


Waarom Exsolvæ?

  • Werken aan projecten met écht impact
  • Een innovatieve omgeving waarin leren en kennisdeling centraal staan
  • Volop kansen om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen
  • Hybride werken; flexibel tussen kantoor en thuis
  • Een competitief salaris met een mooi pakket aan voordelen
  • Klinkt dit als een uitdaging die bij jou past? Laat het me weten en dan vertel ik je er graag meer over!

Title: Data Scientist H/F
Description:
MCA BELGIUM, filiale du groupe français MCA, est une société de conseil en ingénierie et hautes technologies. Avec plus de 15 ans d’expérience en Belgique, nous accompagnons nos clients dans la réalisation de projets industriels complexes dans divers secteurs d’activité (énergie, nucléaire, pharmaceutique, aéronautique, ferroviaire).

Nous rejoindre, c’est intégrer une entreprise dynamique qui place l’accompagnement de ses collaborateurs au cœur de ses priorités. Vous bénéficierez d’un suivi personnalisé et de nombreuses formations pour développer vos compétences.

Votre Rôle

Rattaché(e) au pôle Data & Engineering, vous intervenez sur l’exploitation avancée de données industrielles issues d’équipements d’énergies renouvelables (solaire & éolien), avec pour objectif d’optimiser la performance des installations et d’anticiper les défaillances :


Analyse de données provenant de machines industrielles (production, capteurs, historiques de fonctionnement)
Conception et développement de modèles statistiques et algorithmiques (machine learning, détection d’anomalies)
Identification des pertes de performance, anomalies et risques de panne
Participation à la mise en place de solutions de maintenance prédictive
Contribution à l’optimisation du potentiel des équipements
Restitution des résultats via rapports techniques et documentation associée
Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie


Votre Profil


Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
Maîtrise de Python
Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel


Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.
